children.text: shall be in its entirety fully disclosed, and the full text thereof made available to the appropriate Federal banking agency with supervisory responsibility over the insured depository institution and to the public by each party to the agreement; and
shall obligate each party to comply with this section.

children.heading: Annual report of activity by insured depository institution
children.label: b Annual report of activity by insured depository institution

children.text: Payments, fees, or loans made to any party to the agreement or received from any party to the agreement and the terms and conditions of the same.
Aggregate data on loans, investments, and services provided by each party in its community or communities pursuant to the agreement.
Such other pertinent matters as determined by regulation by the appropriate Federal banking agency with supervisory responsibility over the insured depository institution.

children.heading: Annual report of activity by nongovernmental entities
children.label: c Annual report of activity by nongovernmental entities

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Each nongovernmental entity or person that is not an affiliate of an insured depository institution and that is a party to an agreement described in subsection (a) shall report to the appropriate Federal banking agency with supervisory responsibility over the insured depository institution that is a party to such agreement, not less frequently than once each year, an accounting of the use of funds received pursuant to each such agreement during the preceding 12-month period.

children.heading: Submission to insured depository institution
children.label: 2 Submission to insured depository institution
children.text: A nongovernmental entity or person referred to in paragraph (1) may comply with the reporting requirement in such paragraph by transmitting the report to the insured depository institution that is a party to the agreement, and such insured depository institution shall promptly transmit such report to the appropriate Federal banking agency with supervisory authority over the insured depository institution.

children.heading: Information to be included
children.label: 3 Information to be included
children.text: The accounting referred to in paragraph (1) shall include a detailed, itemized list of the uses to which such funds have been made, including compensation, administrative expenses, travel, entertainment, consulting and professional fees paid, and such other categories, as determined by regulation by the appropriate Federal banking agency with supervisory responsibility over the insured depository institution.

children.heading: Applicability
children.label: d Applicability
children.text: Subsections (b) and (c) shall not apply with respect to any agreement entered into before the end of the 6-month period beginning on .November 12, 1999

children.label: i
children.text: any written contract, written arrangement, or other written understanding that provides for cash payments, grants, or other consideration with a value in excess of $10,000, or for loans the aggregate amount of principal of which exceeds $50,000, annually (or the sum of all such agreements during a 12-month period with an aggregate value of cash payments, grants, or other consideration in excess of $10,000, or with an aggregate amount of loan principal in excess of $50,000); or

children.label: ii
children.text: a group of substantively related contracts with an aggregate value of cash payments, grants, or other consideration in excess of $10,000, or with an aggregate amount of loan principal in excess of $50,000, annually;

children.label: i
children.text: any individual mortgage loan;

children.label: ii
children.text: any specific contract or commitment for a loan or extension of credit to individuals, businesses, farms, or other entities, if the funds are loaned at rates not substantially below market rates and if the purpose of the loan or extension of credit does not include any re-lending of the borrowed funds to other parties; or

children.label: iii
children.text: any agreement entered into by an insured depository institution or affiliate with a nongovernmental entity or person who has not commented on, testified about, or discussed with the institution, or otherwise contacted the institution, concerning the Community Reinvestment Act of 1977 [ et seq.].12 U.S.C. 2901

children.label: A
children.text: to approve or disapprove an application for a deposit facility (as defined in section 803 of the Community Reinvestment Act of 1977 []); or12 U.S.C. 2902

children.label: B
children.text: to assign a rating to an insured depository institution under section 807 of the Community Reinvestment Act of 1977 [].12 U.S.C. 2906

children.heading: Violations by persons other than insured depository institutions or their affiliates
children.label: 1 Violations by persons other than insured depository institutions or their affiliates

children.heading: Material failure to comply
children.label: A Material failure to comply
children.text: If the party to an agreement described in subsection (a) that is not an insured depository institution or affiliate willfully fails to comply with this section in a material way, as determined by the appropriate Federal banking agency, the agreement shall be unenforceable after the offending party has been given notice and a reasonable period of time to perform or comply.

children.heading: Diversion of funds or resources
children.label: B Diversion of funds or resources
i
children.text: Disgorgement by the offending individual of funds received under the agreement.

children.label: ii
children.text: Prohibition of the offending individual from being a party to any agreement described in subsection (a) for a period of not to exceed 10 years.

children.heading: Designation of successor nongovernmental party
children.label: 2 Designation of successor nongovernmental party
children.text: If an agreement described in subsection (a) is found to be unenforceable under this subsection, the appropriate Federal banking agency may assist the insured depository institution in identifying a successor nongovernmental party to assume the responsibilities of the agreement.

children.heading: Inadvertent or de minimis reporting errors
children.label: 3 Inadvertent or de minimis reporting errors
children.text: An error in a report filed under subsection (c) that is inadvertent or de minimis shall not subject the filing party to any penalty.

children.heading: Rule of construction
children.label: g Rule of construction
children.text: No provision of this section shall be construed as authorizing any appropriate Federal banking agency to enforce the provisions of any agreement described in subsection (a).

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: Each appropriate Federal banking agency shall prescribe regulations, in accordance with paragraph (4), requiring procedures reasonably designed to ensure and monitor compliance with the requirements of this section.

children.label: A
children.text: ensure that the regulations prescribed by the agency do not impose an undue burden on the parties and that proprietary and confidential information is protected; and

children.label: B
children.text: establish procedures to allow any nongovernmental entity or person who is a party to a large number of agreements described in subsection (a) to make a single or consolidated filing of a report under subsection (c) to an insured depository institution or an appropriate Federal banking agency.

children.heading: Parties not subject to reporting requirements
children.label: 3 Parties not subject to reporting requirements
A
children.text: to prevent evasions of subsection (e)(1)(B)(iii); and

children.label: B
children.text: to provide further exemptions under such subsection, consistent with the purposes of this section.

children.heading: Coordination, consistency, and comparability
children.label: 4 Coordination, consistency, and comparability
children.text: In carrying out paragraph (1), each appropriate Federal banking agency shall consult and coordinate with the other such agencies for the purposes of assuring, to the extent possible, that the regulations prescribed by each such agency are consistent and comparable with the regulations prescribed by the other such agencies.
